description: Pale pink in color, this wine brings aromas of strawberry Jolly Rancher and watermelon that are followed by a broad off-dry drinking palate. It delivers easy-drinking appeal.
title: Vino La Monarcha 2016 Rosé of Pinot Noir (Ancient Lakes)

description: Fruity and lightly spicy from wood aging, this wine has black fruit aromas. It's lightly tannic, with just enough structure to make it an early-drinking wine. Drink now.
title: Château Haut Peyruguet 2016  Bordeaux

description: Aromas of tropical fruit and banana lead the nose and carry over to the rounded palate along with pineapple and a hint of candied citrus. A note of bitter nut marks the close.
title: Donnachiara 2016  Fiano di Avellino

description: This is a very easy light-bodied red for chilling. The body is dry, slender and fresh. The flavor expression is of red fruit, earth and apple skin. It has a rustic, picnic-style quality. Drink soon.
title: Cave de Hunawihr 2016 Pinot Noir (Alsace)

description: From vineyards on the slope above Cadillac, this is a tight, mineral-textured wine. With crisp lemon flavors, it is brightly lit with acidity and clean fruitiness. The wine is ready to drink.
title: Château Suau 2016  Bordeaux Blanc

description: This ripe fruity wine is bursting with young red fruits. It has a fine structure that gives a core of dryness. It also has a crisp edge that brings out the fruit and the liveliness of this attractive wine. Ready to drink now.
title: Domaine Sauvat 2016 Demoiselles Gamay

description: This wine is fat and slightly oxidative. Bitter-orange flavors and acidity give the wine a tight aftertaste.
title: Casa Agricola Alexandre Relvas 2016 Art. Terra Curtimenta White (Alentejano)

description: Refreshing and vibrant, this wine is delightfully playful in peach, pear and a lingering spectrum of citrus that gives it additional life in the glass. Light, it'll do well on its own or with creamy cheeses or oysters from the sea.
title: Gloria Ferrer 2016 Estate Grown Pinot Blanc (Carneros)

description: Quite dry and flinty, this medium-bodied wine mixes citrus flesh and floral qualities with persistence and texture. Chill it just below cellar temperature, and it will liven up a variety of mellow cheeses and poultry dishes.
title: Bryn Mawr Vineyards 2016 Estate Riesling (Eola-Amity Hills)

description: Crisp and pungent, this has penetrating aromas of cut apple. Flavors resemble fresh, unfermented apple cider. There's a touch of bitterness in the finish.
title: Apolloni 2016 Estate Pinot Grigio (Willamette Valley)

description: A reductive nose distracts from the dusty oak, vanilla and robust peach and papaya notes in this food-friendly, round and approachable wine.
title: Kenwood 2016 Chardonnay (Sonoma County)

description: Broad aromas of melon, salted lemon and soft white peach benefit from a chalky minerality on the nose of this Viognier and Grenache Blanc blend. There is a great sizzle of acid on the sip as well as a grippy texture, with flavors of lemon zest and apricot sitting atop butter and oak influences.
title: Brecon Estate 2016 Viognier-Grenache Blanc (Adelaida District)

description: This medium-pink rosé shows some heft but is mostly about refreshment. Passion fruit and peach notes give way to citrus zest on its crisp, vibrant finish. Drink now.
title: Marrenon 2016 Les Grains Rosé de Merlot (Mediterranée)

description: There is a caramel edge to this ripe wine with its strawberry fruits. It has spice and flavors of vanilla along with the crisp aftertaste of acidity. Drink this Pinot Noir-based wine now.
title: Nicolas Millet 2016 Champ Secret Rosé (Sancerre)

description: The Savigny-lès-Beaune-based producer shows his Pinot Noir skills with this wine. It's generous, with red plum flavor, light wood-aging notes, ripe tannins and crisp acidity. It's rounded and ready to drink.
title: Doudet Naudin 2016 Pinot Noir (Vin de France)

description: The perfume of the Moscatel grape shines through this wine that also has Fernão Pires and Alvarinho in the blend. Ripe and fruity, full of apple and melon flavors, the wine is lightly textured and ready to drink.
title: Quinta do Casal Branco 2016 Lobo e Falcão Branco White (Tejo)

description: Ripe, rich apricot and grapefruit flavors are plush and easy here, but are balanced neatly by fresh acidity and a glimmer of mineral complexity. Delicately textured and brisk on the finish, it's a thrist quenching apéritif to enjoy young.
title: Carl Ehrhard 2016 Rüdesheim Berg Roseneck Kabinett Riesling (Rheingau)

description: The fruit is light and a touch sour, with suggestions of watermelon and berry. It fades into a short finish with a citrusy frame.
title: Lachini 2016 Rosé of Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: This biodynamic estate produces some very fine wines, expressive of their soil types. This still-young wine from volcanic rock is ripe while also intensely structured. It has the potential for great fruitiness while keeping this structure. Drink from 2018.
title: Domaine de l'Ecu 2016 Orthogneiss Sur Lie  (Muscadet Sèvre et Maine)

description: Dusty, minerally white-fruit aromas are generic but appealing. This blend of Viura, Malvasia and Garnacha Blanca has a chunky palate with modest acidity. Green-melon flavors are round but flatten out and turn slightly bitter in front of a basic finish.
title: Olivier Rivière 2016 La Bastid White (Rioja)

description: This delicious wine from old Sémillon vines offers a hint of nuts and spice as well as crisp, clean fruit. It is lively with acidity as well as having a richer side. The wine will be just right from the end of 2017.
title: Château Haut Mouleyre 2016  Bordeaux Blanc

description: The wine is all attractive fruit and crisp acidity. The lemon and ripe apple flavors are fed through this acidity to give a great lift to the creamy texture. Drink from late 2017.
title: Quinta das Marias 2016 Encruzado (Dão)

description: This medium-bodied wine displays a distinctive aroma similar to that of bruised apples. On the palate, it's dry and inoffensive, offering up hints of melon and citrus.
title: Château L'Ermitage 2016 Rosé (Costières de Nîmes)

description: A lively whiff of white blossom, Muscat grapes and lemon enticingly streaks the nose. These flavors continue on a feather-light and dry palate where fresh acidity makes them shine even brighter. This wine is all about expressing the most delicious, dry but totally aromatic lightness. Refreshing and made for summer, it has an irresistible lemon finish.
title: Neumeister 2016 Steirische Klassik Gelber Muskateller

description: This well-oaked, smoky wine is a great value. It has abundant baking spice aromas, as well as rich flavors of ripe pear, nutmeg and vanilla. Ample acidity and delicate tannins help maintain balance.
title: Oak Grove 2016 Family Reserve Chardonnay (California)

description: Very easy-to-like aromas of honeysuckle, jasmine and green tea ice cream show on the nose of this bright and vivacious wine by Dave Potter, one of the forefathers in the evolution of Santa Barbara's Funk Zone neighborhood. A powerful grip grabs hold of the palate, making for a very racy and chalky experience with lots of length, carrying more green-fruit and tea flavors.
title: Municipal Winemakers 2016 Grenache Blanc (Santa Barbara County)

description: One of a pair of wines that comes from specific soil types. This impressive wine, from granite soil, is strongly mineral while also having some great fruit. Apples, pears and green plums all make their presence felt in this wine that deserves aging. Drink from 2018.
title: Domaine de l'Ecu 2016 Granite Sur Lie  (Muscadet Sèvre et Maine)

description: A to Z has almost doubled production of this wine, without a hitch in quality. Floral and citrus components gather strength and roll into ripe tropical flavors: juicy Fruit gum, papaya and melon. It's absolutely yummy and a can't miss value.
title: A to Z 2016 Riesling (Oregon)

description: This vintage is light in color with perfumed fruit and attractive red currant and orange fruit. It is floral, touched by raspberries as well as a strong streak of acidity. This refreshing wine is ready to drink.
title: Ravoire et Fils 2016 Domaine Saint Ferréol Les Vaunières Rosé (Coteaux Varois en Provence)

description: This wine's nose is shy but fresh. The palate shows taut, tart fruit, with hints of red apple and citrus. This is very slender and light with a clean, dry finish.
title: Weixelbaum 2016 Strasser Rosé (Niederösterreich)

description: This old-vine cuvée from one of the region's leading estates boasts aromas of bubble gum and passion fruit. This soon after release, that's not surprising; expect them to settle down into more restrained notes of cherry, melon and passion fruit over the next month or two. It's medium bodied and has a long, mouthwatering finish.
title: Château de Nages 2016 Vieilles Vignes Rosé (Costières de Nîmes)

description: This is a great option for a widely available and affordable rosé. A very pretty pink in color, it shows baked strawberry, rose petals and vanilla ice cream on the nose. The sip tightens around cherry and strawberry-brioche flavors.
title: SeaGlass 2016 Rosé (Monterey County)

description: Fruity and juicy, this wine is open and full of black-cherry flavors. The structure of Morgon waits behind the fruit, giving a dry core. This is a wine for medium-term aging. Drink from 2018.
title: Vignerons de Bel Air 2016 Belairissme Le Py  (Morgon)

description: This is a delicious fruity wine, pink in color and with a swathe of crisp red-currant fruitiness. It is bright with acidity and forward fruit. Drink this attractive wine now.
title: Domaine Laurens 2016 Cuvée Eglantine  (Marcillac)

description: Lemon, orange blossom and rose aromas start this exotic and refreshing wine. On the palate, bright flavors of citrus mingle with melon and pepper. Easygoing but distinctive.
title: Boutari 2016 Moschofilero (Mantinia)

description: Coming from 17-year-old vines, this is a one-of-a-kind wine in the state, seeing no new oak and released earlier than most whites. Light but alluring aromas of fresh herb, sage, flower and raspberry lead to ultra-pure red-fruit flavors. It's delightful on its own but put it on the dinner table to see it truly shine.
title: Savage Grace 2016 Copeland Vineyard Cabernet Franc (Rattlesnake Hills)

description: A touch of green bell pepper and boxwood appears amidst the citrus scent on the nose. The palate majors on crisp lemon tartness, with some of those appetizing green boxwood and pepper flavors, too. The body is light, lean, dry and refreshing.
title: Winzerkeller Andau 2016 Sauvignon Blanc (Burgenland)

description: True ice wines are a rarity in the Northwest. This one brings honeyed aromas of dried pineapple and mango that draw you into the glass. The flavors are sweet and rich but still manage to remain light on their feet. It's a perfect end to any meal.
title: Kiona 2016 Estate Grown Ice Wine Chenin Blanc (Red Mountain)

description: There are extreme amounts of citrus on the nose of this bottling, particularly reminiscent of fresh-squeezed grapefruit juice, as well as an edgy, crushed slate minerality. The grapefruit character explodes on the sip, which is very refreshing, unique and compelling.
title: Woodstock Collection 2016 Misty Roses Rosé (Santa Barbara County)

description: Light, fresh and fruity without being at all sweet or soft, this is a charming dry wine to serve with lunch or at wine o'clock in the afternoon. It smell like freshly cut apples and Bosc pears, tastes crisp and feels tangy and palate cleansing.
title: Andis 2016 Cuvée Blanc White (Amador County)

description: Subtle nutty aromas and flavors like untoasted almonds and cashews meet mild melon notes in this dry, full-bodied and viscous wine. It should pair well with dried, salty cheeses or prosciutto.
title: Deaver 2016 Orange Muscat (Amador County)

description: Mild citrus, melon and peach aromas are clean. This wine feels solid and a touch plump, but with agility. Peach and melon flavors are simple, and it's fresh, tasty and fairly elegant on the finish.
title: Evaia 2016 Rosé (Rioja)

description: Aromas of lemon curd and citrus blossom come with a hint of pine. This feels lemony and narrow, with a tight body that's showing little to no give. Nectarine, citrus and green-melon flavors are fresh prior to a dry citrusy finish.
title: Rosa Nautica 2016 Albariño (Rías Baixas)

description: A pale, dry wine, this is crisp and lightly textured. It has a bright lemon character as well as red-currant fruits. Produced in the hills behind Saint-Tropez it is named after the painter who had strong connections with Provence. Drink this wine now.
title: Vignobles de Saint-Tropez 2016 Domaine Cézanne Rosé (Côtes de Provence)

description: Almost white in color, this is a rich wine with intense red-currant and raspberry fruits. It is a dry wine, destined to partner food, with fine acidity as well as a herbal edge. It is a concentrated wine, ready to drink from the end of 2017.
title: Bernard Magrez 2016 Excellence des Muraires Rosé (Côtes de Provence)

description: Made from 96% Grenache Noir and 4% Syrah, this is a fresh, pretty and easy to like rosé, with attractive notes of peony, strawberry top, orange blossom and thyme. The mouthfeel is light, but flavorful, with a pleasant citrus-pith and watermelon-rind bitterness that lingers on the finish.
title: Penya 2016 Grenache Noir-Syrah Rosé (Côtes Catalanes)

description: Made with 85% Corvina, 10% Rondinella and 5% Corvinone, this opens with aromas of red-skinned berry, clove and a hint of smoke. The savory, easy-drinking palate offers red currant, raspberry jam and white pepper. It's meant to be enjoyed young so drink soon.
title: Zenato 2016  Valpolicella Superiore

description: This has subdued aromas suggesting beeswax and toasted almond. The straightforward palate offers apple and white cherry alongside tangy acidity.
title: Albino Piona 2016  Bardolino Chiaretto

description: This soft, medium-sweet wine comes from one of the oldest producers in the Vinho Verde region, established in 1790. The wine is fruity and crisp, with a light sparkle. Bright lemon acidity comes through strongly at the end.
title: Casa de Vilacetinho 2016 Grande Escolha White (Vinho Verde)

description: Touches of vanilla and spice give this wine an attractive character. Its light, tangy texture adds crispness that supplements the bright acidity. Drink now.
title: M. & J-F Bertrand 2016 Fleur de Carbonnel Chardonnay (Vin de France)

description: Showing aromas of overripe mango, peach, lime and white flowers, this wine is intense on the nose. The palate is equally loud, with lime and candied peach standing out. The acidity is prickly and gives a sour taste to the finish.
title: Blue Valley 2016 Muskat Ottonel (Middleburg)

description: Snappy aromas of citrus fruits and sweat are mildly but noticeably pyrazinic. This feels zesty, with citric acidity as the driving force. Tart limited citrus flavors turn more pithy and bitter on the finish.
title: Domaines Barons de Rothschild (Lafite) 2016 Los Vascos Sauvignon Blanc (Casablanca Valley)

description: This medium-bodied wine boasts aromas reminiscent of trading-card bubble gum. Is that even still a thing? It's vaguely cherry-like, with hints of baby powder, yet completely dry and refreshing, with a crisp, clean finish. Drink now.
title: Yalumba 2016 The Y Series Sangiovese Rosé (South Australia)

description: For the modest tariff, this is really good. Aromas of crushed stone and dried flowers lead the way, followed by ample berry flavors and a medium-bodied, silky feel. Drink now.
title: Boutinot 2016 Les Cerisiers Rosé (Côtes du Rhône)

description: This is a fruity crisp blend of classic Gascony grapes. It has a clean bright texture with just a touch of honey as well as plenty of lemon and tangy orange. Intensely fruity, it is ready to drink.
title: Domaine du Tariquet 2016 Classic White (Côtes de Gascogne)

description: This opens with faint honeysuckle and white stone fruit aromas. The informal, lively palate offers green apple, peach and citrus hints, alongside bright acidity.
title: Tenuta Sant'Antonio 2016 Fontana  (Soave)

description: The wine is soft and warmly ripe with simple raspberry fruitiness. It has a touch of crisp acidity to keep it in order and to help its refreshing character. Drink now.
title: Maison Belle Claire 2016 Rosé (Côtes de Provence)

description: Medicinal berry aromas are outweighed by a strong blast of clove. While this feels wide and chunky, it's also acidic. Following the nose, this tastes heavily of clove along with plum, currant and raspberry. On the finish, there's no shedding that dominant oak-based clove character.
title: Indomita 2016 Gran Reserva Cabernet Sauvignon (Maipo Valley)

description: Sweet, simple and fruity, this light-bodied wine smells floral, tastes appley and feels soft and sugary to sip.
title: Naggiar 2016 Estate Muscat Canelli (Sierra Foothills)

description: A white pepper aroma and full citrus and mineral flavors make this an interesting and multifaceted wine that also refreshes the taste buds. It has good, bracing acidity, medium body and subtle layering.
title: Two Angels 2016 Sauvignon Blanc (High Valley)
